codeReview.summary: The documentation-and-exports feature was successfully implemented with comprehensive JSDoc documentation, README updates, and proper module exports for tree-shaking. All public structured output APIs have thorough JSDoc documentation with examples. The README was updated with a complete Structured Output section including all API patterns, provider compatibility, and usage examples. The separate module import (universal-llm-client/structured-output) is properly configured in package.json exports. All 321 tests pass, typecheck is clean, and build succeeds.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md already documents the JSDoc convention well with examples of comprehensive documentation for public APIs. The worker followed this pattern correctly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md has no skill-specific documentation gaps to address

sharedStateObservations.observation: The README update patterns (features list addition, new section with examples, API reference table update) are consistent with existing documentation style and don't need special codification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: README.md edit shows consistent markdown formatting style

summary: Feature passes review. Worker verified that JSDoc documentation was already comprehensive (from prior features), added comprehensive structured output documentation to README with examples for all four API surfaces (generateStructured, tryParseStructured, chat with output, streaming), updated the Features list and API Reference table, and verified tree-shaking works via the structured-output module export in package.json. The package.json exports configuration correctly provides separate module imports. All validation passed: typecheck (0 errors), tests (321 tests pass), build (produces dist/structured-output.js and dist/structured-output.d.ts). No breaking changes to existing APIs.
